internal-tools.description: Tools built to improve internal workflows, monitoring, and productivity

customer-facing.description: Products or prototypes directly serving end users or merchants

automation.description: Automating repetitive PM workflows and operational tasks

data-analysis.description: Using AI for deep technical analysis and preparation

quotes: Getting access to the right tools was my biggest hurdle. (alexandras)
6 hours of setup struggles — disk space, DevBox, feature gating. (rutvij)
Manual testing the account app on devboxes was painful. (daniel)
I couldn't access the Google Sheets append tool initially — stuck in a loop from not setting up the MCP server correctly. (alexandras)
top_friction_points.suggested_fix: Streamlined MCP setup wizard, pre-configured PM devbox template, better error messages for common setup issues

top_friction_points.issue: AI overconfidence / false claims about capabilities
quotes: Claude claimed it could gate feature flags. It couldn't. Always verify system access claims. (rutvij)
Had to tell Claude not to push without approval repeatedly. (daniel)
Claude over-extracted then under-extracted — needed calibration examples. (alexandras)
top_friction_points.suggested_fix: Better guardrails on tool access claims, explicit capability boundaries in system prompts

quotes: CI autofix awareness — kept regenerating locally instead of pulling CI autofixes. (daniel)
Cursor requires machine to stay awake for long CI cycles. (daniel)
Simple frontend approach beat complex devbox orchestration. (rutvij)
top_friction_points.suggested_fix: Auto-detect CI autofix availability, better devbox setup documentation for PMs, tmux-based workflow recommendation

top_friction_points.issue: Slack API rate limiting and permissions
quotes: Slack API rate limiting was brutal — all optimization attempts failed. (jenlee)
The permissions issues were annoying — had to build a new Slack app and fiddle with OAuth tokens endlessly. (jenlee)
top_friction_points.suggested_fix: Pre-configured Slack app templates for common PM use cases, better rate limit handling in MCP tools

quotes: Ambiguous prompts waste cycles — no channel, no timeframe, no relationship context. (preswick)
Each time we resumed, I had to re-establish context from the transcript. Daniel kept the project's narrative in his head across weeks; I had to reconstruct it each time. (daniel/claude)
top_friction_points.suggested_fix: Better session handoff tooling, structured context templates for multi-session projects

fireside_candidates.author: Jennifer Lee (jenlee)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Highly original concept (Oura ring for work relationships), rich narrative with mock interview format, practical PM tool with distribution potential

fireside_candidates.author: Jennifer Lee (jenlee)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Agent-to-agent commerce with real stablecoin payment — showcases machine payments, onramp, and crypto in one flow. The coffee photo is the perfect punchline.

fireside_candidates.author: Daniel Heffernan (daniel)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Excellent narrative arc (failed approaches → discovery → elegant solution). $0/month cost. Great meta-section on writing the report with AI.

fireside_candidates.author: Daniel Heffernan (daniel)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Real production impact across 8 PRs. Unique 'Claude writes its own retrospective' section. Strongest demonstration of PM-as-EM pattern.

fireside_candidates.author: Robbie Preswick (preswick)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Meta and delightful — agents writing their own 515 and roasting the human. 34 sessions / 9 skills in one week shows the fleet model.

fireside_candidates.author: Dan Stokeley (stokeley)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Full SaaS built in 2-3 hours. Great demo of 'vibe-coded startup' potential. Quotable: 'what I do mostly consists of entering Yes repeatedly'.

fireside_candidates.author: Narek Tamoyan (narek)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Practical improvement to a real pain point (Google Doc bug bash). Chrome extension + dashboard + Jira export in one day.

fireside_candidates.author: Kate Brennan (kateb)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Directly addresses 5% of lost Terminal deals. Built in 90 minutes. Shows PMs can prototype solutions for user jobs-to-be-done.

fireside_candidates.author: Aleksandra Bal (aleksandrabal)
fireside_candidates.rationale: 48 jurisdiction-specific rules with real legal accuracy. Strong demonstration of domain expertise + AI speed.

fireside_candidates.author: Alexandra Scolieri (alexandras)
fireside_candidates.rationale: Reusable skill for ongoing feedback synthesis. Good story about calibration challenges.
